metadata.title: BGP vs Bellman-Ford: Counting to Infinity

beats.narration_text: Halo. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of bgp vs bellman-ford: counting to infinity. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of bgp vs bellman-ford: counting to infinity
output: ✓ BGP vs Bellman-Ford: Counting to Infinity

beats.narration_text: BGP vs Bellman-Ford: Counting to Infinity is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. After a link fails, Bellman-Ford loops forever counting up. Path-vector (BGP) detects the loop in 1 step by checking if its own ID appears in the advertised path.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: BGP vs Bellman-Ford: Counting to Infinity

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of BGP vs Bellman-Ford: Counting to Infinity. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript.  Verify: After a link fails, Bellman-Ford loops forever counting up. Path-vector (BGP) detects the loop in 1 step by checking if . Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of BGP vs Bellman-Ford: Counting to Infinity. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript.  Verify: After a link fails, Bellman-Ford

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.

beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from bgp-counting-to-infinity.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: bgp vs bellman-ford: counting to infinity. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main control. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main control

beats.narration_text: After a link fails, Bellman-Ford loops forever counting up. Path-vector (BGP) detects the loop in 1 step by checking if its own ID appears in the advertised path.. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of bgp vs bellman-ford: counting to infinity. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this BGP vs Bellman-Ford: Counting to Infinity simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
